name: analytics
description: Product analytics via Amplitude, behind a thin app-owned wrapper. Product analytics only — no crash reporting.

notes: Amplitude depends on @react-native-async-storage/async-storage@^1.x. Apps on async-storage 2.x MUST add the npmOverrides above to package.json, or npm installs a duplicate native module (two RNCAsyncStorage copies) that breaks the iOS/Android build.

Add the override BEFORE installing Amplitude. If Amplitude was already installed, the stale lockfile keeps the nested copy: delete the nested lock entry + node_modules/@amplitude/analytics-react-native/node_modules and run `npm install`, then confirm with `npm ls @react-native-async-storage/async-storage` (should show one version, 'deduped').

EXPO_PUBLIC_AMPLITUDE_KEY.prompt: Paste your Amplitude project API key (Amplitude > Settings > Projects > [project] > API Key):
EXPO_PUBLIC_AMPLITUDE_KEY.description: Amplitude project API key. Public client key — safe to ship in the app bundle.

governance.policy: Every PR that adds or changes user-facing behaviour must add/update events (see event-guidelines.md). Refactors, bugfixes, styling, docs, tests, and config are exempt.

agentInstructions.note: Append (never overwrite) to whichever agent-instructions file the app uses; create CLAUDE.md if neither exists. The snippet is fenced with BEGIN/END markers so it can be re-synced in place.

governance.ciCheck: Soft, non-blocking PR reminder (.github/workflows/analytics-check.yml). It cannot verify the correct events were added; human review + the PR checklist are the real gate.

verify: App runs on a dev/standalone build (Amplitude does not work in Expo Go)
The 'App Opened' event appears in Amplitude under User Look-Up / Events within a minute
